creator: kreinholz@gmail.com
text:
Created attachment 252860
Temporary Makefile patch to build devel/protobuf-27.3,1

I had the same build error attempting to build devel/protobuf-27.3,1 on 14.1-STABLE stable/14-n268424-644d81447118 GENERIC amd64, using synth-3.01.

Without troubleshooting the underlying undefined symbol issue, I added the following LD_FLAGS to Makefile:

LDFLAGS=        -Wl,-allow-shlib-undefined

Synth then builds devel/protobuf-27.3,1 without issue. I suspect Poudriere will as well, but I can't really recommend this as anything besides a temporary workaround to build protobuf, since an undefined symbol in libprotoc.so.27.3.0 could cause crashes or odd/unexpected behavior.

This is the error message generated by synth when attempting to build devel/protobuf without my patch:

ld: error: undefined reference due to --no-allow-shlib-undefined: absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage& absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage::operator<<<int, 0>(int const&)
>>> referenced by libprotoc.so.27.3.0

ld: error: undefined reference due to --no-allow-shlib-undefined: absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage& absl::lts_20240722::log_internal::LogMessage::operator<<<unsigned long, 0>(unsigned long const&)
>>> referenced by libprotoc.so.27.3.0
c++: error: linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation)
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.
===> Compilation failed unexpectedly.
Try to set MAKE_JOBS_UNSAFE=yes and rebuild before reporting the failure to
the maintainer.
*** Error code 1
